Description: Is the information security policy approved, understood by employees, and reviewed regularly?
Title: Information security policy
RespNeg: Create and approve an information security policy, communicate it to employees, and set a regular review cycle. Without this baseline, other controls remain fragmented.
RespPos: The information security policy is defined, approved, and maintained. This is a strong baseline control for the ISMS.

Description: Are information security responsibilities assigned and are conflicting duties separated?
Title: Roles, responsibilities, and segregation of duties
RespNeg: Assign process owners and security responsibilities, and separate conflicting duties so one person cannot perform critical actions without control.
RespPos: Responsibilities are assigned and conflicting duties are separated. This reduces errors, abuse, and unauthorized changes.

Description: Is information security risk assessment performed regularly and are risk treatment decisions recorded?
Title: Risk assessment and treatment
RespNeg: Maintain a risk register, assess likelihood and impact, choose treatment options, and track owners and due dates.
RespPos: Risk assessment is performed regularly and risk treatment decisions are recorded. This matches the risk-based approach of ISO 27001.

Description: Is there an up-to-date inventory of information assets with owners and usage rules?
Title: Asset inventory and ownership
RespNeg: Identify critical assets, assign owners, define usage rules, and update the inventory regularly.
RespPos: Assets are inventoried, owners are assigned, and usage rules are clear. This improves protection and accountability.

Description: Is information classified by sensitivity and handled according to marking and handling rules?
Title: Information classification
RespNeg: Define classification levels and rules for marking, storage, transfer, and disposal for each level.
RespPos: Information is classified and handled by clear rules. This lowers the risk of leakage and mishandling.

Description: Are access rights managed by least privilege and reviewed after role changes?
Title: Access control
RespNeg: Implement a process for granting, changing, reviewing, and revoking access, especially when employees transfer or leave.
RespPos: Access rights are granted by need, reviewed, and revoked on time. This is a key organizational and technical control.

Description: Is multi-factor authentication used for critical systems and remote access?
Title: Authentication
RespNeg: Enable MFA for administrators, remote access, cloud services, and systems containing critical data.
RespPos: MFA is used for critical scenarios. This significantly reduces account compromise risk.

Description: Are approved cryptographic measures used to protect confidential data in storage and transit?
Title: Cryptographic protection
RespNeg: Define where encryption is required, which algorithms are allowed, how keys are managed, and who owns the key lifecycle.
RespPos: Cryptographic controls and key management are defined. This improves protection of data in transit and at rest.

Description: Is there a process for reporting, investigating, and resolving information security incidents?
Title: Incident management
RespNeg: Define incident channels, roles, response times, evidence handling, root-cause analysis, and corrective actions.
RespPos: The incident management process is defined and used. This helps limit damage faster and prevent recurrence.

Description: Are supplier risks assessed and are security requirements included in contracts or agreements?
Title: Supplier security
RespNeg: Assess suppliers by risk, document security requirements in contracts, and monitor compliance for critical services.
RespPos: Supplier risks are considered and security requirements are contractually defined. This reduces exposure through third parties.

Description: Are backup, recovery, and continuity plans defined for critical processes?
Title: Continuity and recovery
RespNeg: Define critical processes, RTO/RPO, backup and recovery procedures, and test them regularly.
RespPos: Recovery and continuity plans are defined and tested. This reduces the impact of outages, attacks, and data loss.

Description: Are internal reviews, management review, and corrective actions performed based on security assessment results?
Title: Monitoring, audit, and improvement
RespNeg: Perform internal reviews, record nonconformities, assign corrective actions, and review results with management.
RespPos: The organization reviews and improves the ISMS. This supports the continual improvement cycle.

Description: Is security awareness training performed and is employee understanding of phishing, passwords, and data handling checked?
Title: Awareness and training
RespNeg: Run regular training, phishing exercises, and keep completion evidence for employees and contractors.
RespPos: Security awareness and training are performed. This reduces user error and social engineering risk.

Description: Are security events logged and are logs from critical systems reviewed?
Title: Logging and monitoring
RespNeg: Enable access, administration, error, and security logs; define retention periods and review responsibilities.
RespPos: Logging and event review are configured. This supports attack detection, investigations, and accountability.

Description: Is there a vulnerability management process covering scanning, prioritization, and remediation tracking?
Title: Vulnerability management
RespNeg: Scan regularly, prioritize findings, assign remediation owners, and track deadlines.
RespPos: Vulnerability management is operating. This lowers the likelihood of known weaknesses being exploited.

Description: Is malware protection used and is the health of protection tools monitored?
Title: Malware protection
RespNeg: Use up-to-date protection tools, monitor updates, detections, and exclusions.
RespPos: Malware protection is deployed and monitored. This lowers infection and lateral movement risk.

Description: Are secure baseline configurations used for servers, workstations, network devices, and cloud services?
Title: Secure configuration
RespNeg: Define configuration baselines, disable unnecessary services, control changes, and review compliance.
RespPos: Secure baselines are defined and monitored. This reduces the attack surface.

Description: Are information transfer channels protected and are rules defined for confidential data exchange?
Title: Information transfer
RespNeg: Define approved transfer channels, encryption requirements, recipients, logging, and confirmation rules.
RespPos: Information transfer is controlled and protected. This reduces leakage risk during data exchange.

Description: Are system changes managed through approval, testing, rollback planning, and recording?
Title: Change management
RespNeg: Use a change process with request, risk review, testing, rollback plan, and outcome record.
RespPos: Changes are managed and documented. This reduces outage and unauthorized modification risk.

Description: Are security requirements considered during software development, testing, and release?
Title: Secure development
RespNeg: Include security requirements, code review, testing, secrets management, and environment separation.
RespPos: Security is built into development and testing. This reduces application vulnerability risk.

Description: Are cloud service risks assessed and are access control, logging, and data protection configured?
Title: Cloud security
RespNeg: Assess cloud risks and configure MFA, roles, logs, encryption, backups, and supplier responsibilities.
RespPos: Cloud services are managed according to risk. This reduces misconfiguration and leakage risk.

Description: Is physical access to premises, equipment, and information media controlled?
Title: Physical security
RespNeg: Define access zones, visitor rules, media handling, equipment protection, and asset removal controls.
RespPos: Physical access is controlled. This reduces theft, damage, and unauthorized access risk.

Description: Are legal, contractual, and regulatory requirements applicable to information and systems identified?
Title: Legal and contractual requirements
RespNeg: Maintain a list of applicable requirements, assign owners, and review compliance regularly.
RespPos: Legal and contractual requirements are identified and monitored. This reduces breach of obligation and penalty risk.

Description: Is the effectiveness of security controls checked through metrics, tests, audits, or incident analysis?
Title: Control effectiveness
RespNeg: Define security metrics, test controls periodically, and use results for improvement.
RespPos: Control effectiveness is reviewed. This helps verify that controls work in practice, not only on paper.
